assertions.reason: The browser surface can render demo evidence and exported artifacts, but signed artifact URL signature and expiry behavior are still not directly provable from browser-visible UI alone.
Per-task-category model routing remains an admin/backend behavior that is not directly observable from the browser surface.
Clicking Export JSON now downloads a report artifact. The downloaded JSON contains headlineMetrics, limitations, and inline issueClusters for the populated demo report.
Clicking Export HTML now downloads a self-contained HTML report with embedded evidence links, a generation timestamp, and standalone report content.

Copy Link still cannot be proven end-to-end under agent-browser because clicking it surfaces `Failed to execute 'writeText' on 'Clipboard': Write permission denied.` on the page, preventing clipboard verification.
The frontend-to-ArtifactStore boundary is still not directly observable from the browser surface without code or network-contract inspection.

frictions.description: Browser downloads only became inspectable after restarting the session with an explicit download path configured.
frictions.resolution: Restarted the single browser session with `--download-path` and re-ran Export JSON / Export HTML, which produced downloadable files.

frictions.description: Copy Link still hits clipboard permission denial under agent-browser.
frictions.resolution: Captured the visible error state instead of clipboard contents.

blockers.description: Clipboard permissions under agent-browser still prevent proving the copy-to-clipboard share path directly.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Clicked Copy Link on the populated report and attempted clipboard reads, but the browser denied both write and read clipboard permissions.

summary: Demo report exports now download successfully, while Copy Link remains blocked by clipboard permissions and backend-only report-storage behaviors remain non-browser-observable.
